title: Manage dependencies and targets with Swift Package Manager
description: Define products, targets, and version-pinned dependencies in Package.swift and resolve a committed Package.resolved for reproducible builds.
body: Steps: 1. Declare dependencies in Package.swift with explicit version rules (.upToNextMajor(from:) or an exact range), not a floating branch. 2. Split code into targets with clear dependsOn edges and a matching test target. 3. Run `swift package resolve` and commit Package.resolved so every machine builds the same revisions. 4. Build and test with `swift build` / `swift test`. 5. Update intentionally with `swift package update` and review the resolved diff. Rollback point: before changing a dependency's major-version rule. Validation: Package.resolved is byte-stable across machines and swift build succeeds from a clean checkout. Risk boundary: pinning to a branch instead of a version makes builds non-reproducible and can pull breaking changes silently; record the version rule and escalate before depending on an unreleased branch.
